# LinkedIn Launch Posts

## Post 1 — The Big Launch

**Headline:** I spent months building the #1 system design resource on GitHub — here's why.

Every senior engineer I know has the same pain point: system design is hard to learn systematically.

There are great blog posts, amazing conference talks, and fantastic books — but nothing that connects all the dots from "what is CAP theorem" to "how do I lead a postmortem as Staff Engineer."

So I built it.

**System Design Mastery** — an open-source handbook on GitHub with 25 modules, 450+ topics, and 550+ Mermaid diagrams:

🔹 01-04: Foundations (CS → Networking → Linux → Databases)
🔹 05-07: Core Design (System Design → Distributed Systems → Microservices)
🔹 08-09: Containers (Docker → Kubernetes)
🔹 10-12: Cloud (AWS 42 topics × Azure 25 × GCP 26)
🔹 13-17: Practice (Terraform → DevOps → SRE → Security → Observability)
🔹 18-20: Application (22 case studies → 19 projects → 26 interview problems)
🔹 21: Staff Engineer (strategy, mentorship, incident leadership)
🔹 22-25: Specialization (AI/ML, API Design, Testing, Clean Architecture)

Every file follows a consistent format: What is it → Why it was created → When to use it → Architecture (with Mermaid) → Hands-on example → Best practices → Interview questions.

**https://github.com/ajju853/SystemDesignHandbook**

If you find it useful, a star on GitHub helps others discover it. Contributions and feedback are very welcome.

What topics are missing from your experience?

---

## Post 2 — The AI/ML Module

**Headline:** System design is changing — here's what I'm adding for the AI era.

Most system design resources still don't cover AI infrastructure. That's a gap I wanted to fill.

In my open-source handbook, I added a dedicated **AI/ML System Design** module covering:

▪️ Transformer architecture (attention, KV cache, RoPE, GQA)
▪️ RAG architecture (chunking, embedding, hybrid search, reranking)
▪️ Vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus, FAISS, HNSW)
▪️ Model serving (vLLM, TGI, Triton, TensorRT-LLM)
▪️ Prompt engineering patterns (few-shot, CoT, ReAct, DSPy)
▪️ AI agent architectures (tool use, function calling, multi-agent)
▪️ GPU optimization (Flash Attention, PagedAttention, quantization)
▪️ Cost optimization (caching, distillation, pruning, spot instances)

Each topic includes a Mermaid diagram, hands-on example, interview questions, and real company usage.

Because whether you're a Staff Engineer or an IC, AI infrastructure is becoming table stakes.

**https://github.com/ajju853/SystemDesignHandbook**

What AI/ML topics should I add next?
